exercises.title: How many centers in 'abba'?
exercises.instructions:
For string s='abba' (length 4):
A: How many total expansion centers?
B: Which center produces the longest palindrome?
C: What is the longest palindromic substring?
exercises.expected_solution:
A: 2n-1 = 2(4)-1 = 7 total centers
   4 odd centers (single chars): indices 0(a), 1(b), 2(b), 3(a)
   3 even centers (between chars): (0,1) (1,2) (2,3)

B: Even center between indices 1 and 2 (between the two b's)
   Expand: s[1]='b'==s[2]='b' → match, expand
           s[0]='a'==s[3]='a' → match, expand
           lo=-1 or hi=4 → stop
   Result: entire string 'abba'

C: 'abba' — length 4, the entire string
   Note: The two odd centers at 'b' (index 1 and 2) produce 'b' (length 1).
   The even center between them produces 'abba' (length 4).
   Always check BOTH odd and even centers!

test_cases.expected:
A: 2n-1 = 2(4)-1 = 7 total centers
   4 odd centers (single chars): indices 0(a), 1(b), 2(b), 3(a)
   3 even centers (between chars): (0,1) (1,2) (2,3)

B: Even center between indices 1 and 2 (between the two b's)
   Expand: s[1]='b'==s[2]='b' → match, expand
           s[0]='a'==s[3]='a' → match, expand
           lo=-1 or hi=4 → stop
   Result: entire string 'abba'

C: 'abba' — length 4, the entire string
   Note: The two odd centers at 'b' (index 1 and 2) produce 'b' (length 1).
   The even center between them produces 'abba' (length 4).
   Always check BOTH odd and even centers!

hints: 2n-1 centers for length-n string
Even centers: 'abba' has center between the two b's
Always check both odd (single char) and even (between chars) centers

exercises.title: isPalindrome edge cases
exercises.instructions:
Predict true/false for each:
A: isPalindrome('')
B: isPalindrome('a')
C: isPalindrome('aa')
D: isPalindrome('ab')
E: isPalindrome('A man a plan a canal Panama') ← case-insensitive, ignore non-alpha
exercises.expected_solution:
A: '' (empty string) → true — vacuously palindrome, no mismatches
B: 'a' → true — single char always palindrome
C: 'aa' → true — first==last, expand, lo>hi, done
D: 'ab' → false — 'a'≠'b', mismatch at first comparison
E: 'A man a plan a canal Panama' (ignore non-alpha, lowercase) → true
   Cleaned: 'amanaplanacanalpanama'
   Is this a palindrome? Let's check:
   a m a n a p l a n a c a n a l p a n a m a
   Reversed: a m a n a p l a n a c a n a l p a n a m a
   Yes — classic palindrome sentence!

test_cases.expected:
A: '' (empty string) → true — vacuously palindrome, no mismatches
B: 'a' → true — single char always palindrome
C: 'aa' → true — first==last, expand, lo>hi, done
D: 'ab' → false — 'a'≠'b', mismatch at first comparison
E: 'A man a plan a canal Panama' (ignore non-alpha, lowercase) → true
   Cleaned: 'amanaplanacanalpanama'
   Is this a palindrome? Let's check:
   a m a n a p l a n a c a n a l p a n a m a
   Reversed: a m a n a p l a n a c a n a l p a n a m a
   Yes — classic palindrome sentence!

hints: Empty string: no mismatches → palindrome
Single char: trivially palindrome
'A man...Panama': ignoring spaces/punctuation, case-insensitive

exercises.title: Valid Palindrome (LeetCode #125)
exercises.instructions:
Check if string is palindrome after lowercasing and removing non-alphanumeric.
'A man, a plan, a canal: Panama' → true
'race a car' → false

O(n) two pointers, O(1) space.
hints: Skip non-alphanumeric with inner while
toLowerCase() for case-insensitive
O(1) space: only lo and hi variables

exercises.title: Longest Palindromic Substring (LeetCode #5)
exercises.instructions:
Find longest palindromic substring.
'babad' → 'bab' or 'aba'
'cbbd' → 'bb'
'a' → 'a'

Expand around center: O(n²) time O(1) space.
hints: expand(s,i,i): odd length. expand(s,i,i+1): even length
After expand: s.substring(lo+1, hi) — indices after the loop
Try both for each center, track longest

exercises.title: Valid Palindrome II (LeetCode #680)
exercises.instructions:
Check if string can become palindrome by removing at most ONE character.
'aba' → true (already palindrome)
'abca' → true (remove 'c' → 'aba')
'abc' → false

Two pointer: on mismatch, try deleting either left or right char.
hints: On mismatch: try removing s[lo] (check lo+1,hi) OR removing s[hi] (check lo,hi-1)
check(): simple two-pointer palindrome in range [lo,hi]
Only ONE deletion allowed — check two possibilities on first mismatch

exercises.title: Count palindromic substrings (LeetCode #647)
exercises.instructions:
Count all palindromic substrings in s.
's=abc' → 3 (a, b, c)
's=aaa' → 6 (a,a,a,aa,aa,aaa)

Expand around center: each valid expansion = 1 palindrome.
hints: Each successful expansion = 1 palindrome (count++)
Both odd and even centers at each position
'aaa': 3 singles + 2 doubles + 1 triple = 6

exercises.title: Palindrome Partitioning (LeetCode #131)
exercises.instructions:
Return all partitions of s where every substring is palindrome.
's=aab' → [['a','a','b'],['aa','b']]
's=a' → [['a']]
hints: Backtrack: try each prefix, if palindrome → recurse on rest
isPalin: two-pointer O(k)
Prune: only recurse when prefix is palindrome

exercises.title: Minimum cuts for palindrome partitioning (LeetCode #132)
exercises.instructions:
Find minimum cuts to partition string into all palindromic substrings.
's=aab' → 1 (cut between a and b: 'aa'+'b')
's=a' → 0
's=ab' → 1

DP: minCuts[i] = min cuts for s[0..i].
hints: Precompute isPalin[i][j]: O(n²) using expand or DP
cuts[i]: if isPalin[0][i] → 0. Else: min over j where isPalin[j][i]: cuts[j-1]+1
Two-pass: precompute palindromes, then compute cuts

exercises.title: Longest Palindromic Subsequence (LeetCode #516)
exercises.instructions:
Find length of longest palindromic subsequence.
's=bbbab' → 4 ('bbbb')
's=cbbd' → 2 ('bb')

Key: LPS(s) = LCS(s, reverse(s))
hints: LPS(s) = LCS(s, rev(s)) — elegant insight
Same LCS recurrence: match→diagonal+1, no-match→max(above,left)
Min insertions to make palindrome = n - LPS(s)

exercises.title: Expand around center on 'racecar'
exercises.instructions:
Trace expand-around-center on s='racecar' (n=7).
Which center produces the longest palindrome?
What is the answer?
exercises.expected_solution:
s = 'racecar' (n=7)
Total centers: 2×7-1 = 13

Odd centers at each index:
  i=0: 'r' → expand: r alone → 'r' (len 1)
  i=1: 'a' → r≠a → 'a' (len 1)
  i=2: 'c' → a≠e → 'c' (len 1)
  i=3: 'e' → c==c → expand → a==a → expand → r==r → expand → OOB
             → 'racecar' (len 7) ← WINNER!
  i=4: 'c' → e≠e? Actually e==e → 'ece' → a==a? → c≠a → 'ece' (len 3)
       Wait: s[3]='e', s[4]='c': expand(4,4): s[3]='e', s[5]='a': e≠a → 'c' (len 1)
  i=5: 'a' → c≠r → 'a' (len 1)
  i=6: 'r' → a≠? → 'r' (len 1)

Even centers: all have mismatch immediately (racecar has odd total length)

Answer: 'racecar' itself — length 7, center at index 3 ('e')
'racecar' is a palindrome!

test_cases.expected:
s = 'racecar' (n=7)
Total centers: 2×7-1 = 13

Odd centers at each index:
  i=0: 'r' → expand: r alone → 'r' (len 1)
  i=1: 'a' → r≠a → 'a' (len 1)
  i=2: 'c' → a≠e → 'c' (len 1)
  i=3: 'e' → c==c → expand → a==a → expand → r==r → expand → OOB
             → 'racecar' (len 7) ← WINNER!
  i=4: 'c' → e≠e? Actually e==e → 'ece' → a==a? → c≠a → 'ece' (len 3)
       Wait: s[3]='e', s[4]='c': expand(4,4): s[3]='e', s[5]='a': e≠a → 'c' (len 1)
  i=5: 'a' → c≠r → 'a' (len 1)
  i=6: 'r' → a≠? → 'r' (len 1)

Even centers: all have mismatch immediately (racecar has odd total length)

Answer: 'racecar' itself — length 7, center at index 3 ('e')
'racecar' is a palindrome!

hints: Center at 'e' (index 3) expands to entire string
racecar: r-a-c-e-c-a-r → symmetric around 'e'
Palindrome strings: expand at true center gives whole string

exercises.title: Palindrome check with Manacher's O(n) intuition
exercises.instructions:
Implement the O(n) Manacher's algorithm for palindrome radii.
Return array p[] where p[i] = radius of longest palindrome centered at i in transformed string.

Transformed: 'abc' → '#a#b#c#' (handles even/odd uniformly)

Only implement the key idea: use previously computed palindromes to skip recomputation.
hints: Transform to unified odd-length: insert '#' between chars and at ends
mirror = 2*center - i: mirror of i around current center
Use mirror's palindrome radius (capped at right boundary) to skip expansion

exercises.title: Palindrome Pairs (LeetCode #336)
exercises.instructions:
Given list of unique words, find all pairs (i,j) where words[i]+words[j] is palindrome.

words=['abcd','dcba','lls','s','sssll'] → [[0,1],[1,0],[3,2],[2,4]]

For each word: check reverse in set. Handle prefix/suffix palindrome cases.
hints: Split each word: prefix + suffix at every position
If suffix is palindrome: need reverse(prefix) as another word
If prefix is palindrome: need reverse(suffix) as another word

exercises.title: Minimum insertions to make string palindrome
exercises.instructions:
Minimum insertions to make s a palindrome.
's=zzazz' → 0 (already palindrome)
's=mbadm' → 2 (result: mdbadbm or mbdadbm)
's=leetcode' → 5

Answer = n - LPS(s) (where LPS = Longest Palindromic Subsequence)
hints: LPS = Longest Palindromic Subsequence = LCS(s, rev(s))
Min insertions = n - LPS: chars not in LPS need a mirror inserted
zzazz: LPS=5, insertions=0. mbadm: LPS=3, insertions=2

exercises.title: Palindrome rearrangement check
exercises.instructions:
Given string, check if any permutation of its characters can form a palindrome.
's=carrace' → true (can be 'racecar')
's=code' → false (two chars with odd frequency)

Rule: at most one char can have odd frequency.

expected_stdout_contains: at most 1 char with odd frequency

hints: Palindrome: each char appears even times (except middle char in odd-length)
Count chars with odd frequency
At most 1 odd-frequency char → can form palindrome

exercises.title: Complete M31 T3 Palindrome Synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build palindrome toolkit:
1. isPalindrome(s): O(1) space two-pointer
2. longestPalinSubstring(s): expand around center O(n²)
3. countPalinSubstrings(s): count all palindromic substrings
4. longestPalinSubsequence(s): LCS with reverse O(n²)
5. All 4 tests pass
hints: isPalindrome: two-pointer O(1) space
longestPalin: expand odd+even, track longest
longestPalinSubsequence: LCS(s, rev(s))
